title: Syracuse’s cheap municipal internet service now available in more neighborhoods
summary: Syracuse has expanded its municipal Surge Link broadband network to six additional neighborhoods following a $10.8M state grant, building on a pilot launched in October 2023. The service offers 100 Mbps at $14.99/month for low-income residents and $37/month market rate, positioning the city to reach 7,200+ households citywide as part of Mayor Walsh's poverty-reduction strategy.

political_signal.description: Mayor Walsh using municipal broadband expansion as signature poverty-reduction and digital equity initiative; bipartisan federal ARPA funding leveraged with state grants demonstrates executive commitment to closing digital divide.

processing_notes: Article references multiple related city issues (I-81 director, evictions, SU land use, police/fire HQ) but those are separate stories; focus here is Surge Link only.
Jeremy Boyer is consistent city reporter on municipal tech/infrastructure; reliable source.
No direct CIO attribution in article—initiative appears to operate under Mayor's office with external vendor (Community Broadband Networks) managing operations.
Mechanical extraction validated: entities include Mayor, vendor, and system name; departments inferred as Mayor's office (no explicit CIO or IT department referenced).
Pricing comparison to Spectrum included; competitive positioning suggests market-rate sustainability strategy.
PROVISIONAL TAG USED: None required; all tags within available set.
